stem: A public health study surveys 25 residents surveyed and observes 11 positive outcomes out of 25 (44.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (9 observations, rate = 33.3%) and Group B (16 observations, rate = 50.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 34% and 53% of the population. Estimate the true vaccination coverage and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.44. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.4226 (range [0.4067, 0.4402]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.2667, 0.6293]. Point estimate: 0.4226.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.44: 0.44×0.324 + 0.56×0.5 = 0.4226.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
